inclusion_criteria: Randomized or non-randomized interventional studies (including controlled cohorts) that compare weight outcomes between adults with T2DM and adults without T2DM under the same intervention
Adults (≥18 years) with obesity, with baseline BMI in the approximate range of 35.5–42.6 kg/m² (class II–III), including both with and without established type 2 diabetes mellitus
Total diet replacement using liquid-formula diets prescribed as very low calorie (<800 kcal/day) or low-energy (>800 kcal/day) with identical regimens in both diabetes-status groups
Quantitative weight outcomes reported in kilograms (final weight loss and/or rate of weight loss in kg/week) at the end of the active diet phase
exclusion_criteria: No direct comparison by diabetes status (e.g., only T2DM or only non-diabetic participants) or population not obese adults
Interventions not using total diet replacement liquid-formula diets, or diets combined with bariatric surgery or weight-loss pharmacotherapy during the active diet phase
Non-interventional designs without a comparator (e.g., uncontrolled case series), reviews, protocols, editorials, conference abstracts, or non–peer-reviewed reports
Missing quantitative weight outcomes in kilograms
screening_criteria.description: Screening focuses on adult obese populations with and without T2DM undergoing identical total diet replacement liquid-formula diets (VLCD <800 kcal/day or low-energy >800 kcal/day), requiring interventional comparative designs and quantitative weight loss outcomes.

included_articles.title: Less fat reduction per unit weight loss in type 2 diabetic compared with nondiabetic obese individuals completing a very-low-calorie diet program.
